evals.prompt: Extract the concentration and phi fields from my simulation output at evals/files/field_output.json. First read the file to see which fields actually exist, then extract every field that is present and report shape, min, max, and mean for each.
evals.expected_output: Agent inspects evals/files/field_output.json, finds that only 'phi' and 'concentration' fields exist (there is NO temperature field, and the grid is 10x10 = 100 cells), then runs field_extractor.py to extract both fields with per-field statistics.
assertions: Invokes scripts/field_extractor.py with --input evals/files/field_output.json --field 'phi,concentration' --json
Reports that the file contains exactly two fields, phi and concentration, and does NOT invent a temperature field
Reports phi count=100 (a 10x10 grid), phi min=0.0, phi max=1.0, and phi mean approximately 0.3288
Reports concentration min=0.1, concentration max=0.68, and concentration mean approximately 0.3886

script_checks.description: field_extractor reports the real phi and concentration statistics on the pinned fixture

evals.prompt: Did my simulation reach steady state? The residual history is in evals/files/history.json. Use an absolute tolerance of 1e-6 and check the residual quantity. Report whether the 1e-6 threshold was actually reached.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs time_series_analyzer.py on the residual quantity with both --detect-steady-state and --absolute-threshold 1e-6. It reports that the residual decreased monotonically from 1e-2 to a FINAL value of 5e-6, so the 1e-6 absolute threshold was NOT reached (convergence_threshold.reached=false) and the relative steady_state.reached=false, but convergence.type='fast', so the run is converging fast yet has not crossed the 1e-6 target.
assertions: Invokes scripts/time_series_analyzer.py with --input evals/files/history.json --quantity residual and uses --absolute-threshold 1e-6 (the physically correct residual test)
States that the 1e-6 absolute threshold was NOT reached because the final residual is 5e-6 (convergence_threshold.reached=false); does not falsely claim 1e-6 was reached
Reports steady_state.reached=false and convergence.type='fast' for the monotonically decreasing residual
Reports the final residual value of 5e-6 from the history series

script_checks.description: absolute-threshold mode shows the 1e-6 target is NOT reached (final residual 5e-6)
relative steady-state test reports not-reached for the still-decreasing residual

evals.prompt: Compute the volume fraction of the solid phase (phi > 0.5) and the interface area from evals/files/field_output.json using the phi field with threshold 0.5.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs derived_quantities.py twice on evals/files/field_output.json: once for volume_fraction (result 0.32, 32 of 100 cells above 0.5) and once for interface_area (interface_length 2.4 on the 10x10 grid).
assertions: Invokes scripts/derived_quantities.py with --input evals/files/field_output.json --quantity volume_fraction --field phi --threshold 0.5 --json
Invokes scripts/derived_quantities.py with --input evals/files/field_output.json --quantity interface_area --field phi --threshold 0.5 --json
Reports volume_fraction=0.32 (32 of 100 cells with phi above 0.5)
Reports interface_length approximately 2.4 from the marching-squares approximation

script_checks.description: volume fraction of phi>0.5 on the pinned 10x10 fixture
interface length from marching-squares approximation on the same fixture

evals.prompt: Compare my simulation profile at evals/files/profile.json against the analytical solution in evals/files/analytical.json using L2 error and RMSE metrics, and tell me how good the agreement is.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs comparison_tool.py to compute l2_error and rmse between the two pinned profile files, reporting l2_error approximately 0.00458 (interpretation 'excellent', well under 1%) and rmse approximately 0.00176.
assertions: Invokes scripts/comparison_tool.py with --simulation evals/files/profile.json --reference evals/files/analytical.json and the l2_error and rmse metrics --json
Reports l2_error approximately 0.00458 with interpretation 'excellent' (under 1%)
Reports rmse approximately 0.00176
Interprets the agreement as excellent given the L2 error is well below 1%

script_checks.description: comparison_tool computes the real l2_error and rmse on the pinned profiles

evals.prompt: I need a line profile of the phi field along the x-axis at y=0.5 from my 2D simulation output at evals/files/field_output.json. Extract 200 points from x=0 to x=1.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs profile_extractor.py to extract a 200-point 1D line profile of phi along y=0.5; the profile rises from 0.02 at the edges to 1.0 in the center, with mean approximately 0.6877.
assertions: Invokes scripts/profile_extractor.py with --input evals/files/field_output.json --field phi --start '0,0.5,0' --end '1,0.5,0' --points 200 --json
Requests exactly 200 sample points along a horizontal line at y=0.5 from x=0 to x=1
Reports the profile spans phi min=0.02 to max=1.0 with mean approximately 0.6877
Output includes the 1D profile values (200 points) suitable for plotting

script_checks.description: profile_extractor returns 200 points with the real phi profile statistics
